title: PV Sindhu Conquers Japan Open, Ends Two-Year Title Drought
indian_angle: This marks a significant return to form for India's badminton star PV Sindhu, securing her first title in two years.
summary: India's PV Sindhu clinched the Japan Open title, defeating home favourite Akane Yamaguchi in straight games. This victory ends a nearly two-year title drought for the former world champion, signaling a strong comeback.

title: Sindhu Believed in Comeback Amidst Doubts, Says Japan Open Win Not Over Yet
indian_angle: PV Sindhu shares her personal journey of resilience and self-belief during her two-year title drought, offering inspiration to Indian fans.
summary: Following her Japan Open triumph, PV Sindhu revealed her struggle with doubts and questions about her career, emphasizing her unwavering belief in a comeback. She views this victory as a testament to her perseverance and a sign of more to come.

title: Gopichand Hails PV Sindhu's New Surge, Says She Can Beat Top Players
indian_angle: National coach Pullela Gopichand's endorsement highlights the significance of PV Sindhu's return to form for Indian badminton.
summary: Pullela Gopichand expressed confidence in PV Sindhu's renewed form, stating that she now possesses the ability to defeat top-ranked players, signaling a strong outlook for her future performances.

title: Delhi World Championships Venue Undergoing Major Revamp
indian_angle: Preparations for the BWF World Championships 2026 in Delhi are underway, addressing previous venue issues to ensure a world-class event in India.
summary: The venue for the upcoming BWF World Championships 2026 in Delhi is undergoing a significant revamp to prevent issues like bird droppings and stray animals, which caused embarrassment during the recent India Open.

title: BAI Implements 3x15 Scoring System for Domestic Tournaments
indian_angle: This new scoring system will significantly impact how domestic tournaments and national championships are played across India, especially for junior categories.
summary: The Badminton Association of India (BAI) has announced the implementation of a 3x15 scoring system for all domestic ranking tournaments and national championships in the U-13, U-15, and U-17 age groups, aiming to debut it soon.
